No useful hits from paper search. The mechanistic principle is well established in Cohen/Meisl/Knowles Aβ42 kinetics: monomer-sequestering inhibitors reduce the free reactive pool, which lowers both the growth rate and the final fibril mass (since total mass is monomer-conserved). This directly supports coupling the driving term and the capacity ceiling through one sequestration factor.
